description: Build an OCR CLI tool with local (HuggingFace) and LLM-based extraction modes, outputting text to a file with JSON-structured CLI responses.

userStories.title: Bootstrap monorepo workspace and cli_common package
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the monorepo workspace configured with a root pyproject.toml and the cli_common shared package scaffolded so that tool packages can depend on shared models and utilities.
acceptanceCriteria: Root `pyproject.toml` exists with uv workspace config pointing to `packages/*`
`packages/cli_common/pyproject.toml` exists with dependencies: pydantic>=2.0, typer>=0.9
`packages/cli_common/src/cli_common/__init__.py` exists and is importable
`packages/cli_common/src/cli_common/models.py` defines `ToolResponse` and `ToolError` pydantic models matching the JSON envelope pattern from CODING_RULES.md
`packages/cli_common/src/cli_common/errors.py` defines a `ToolException` base exception class
`packages/cli_common/src/cli_common/io.py` defines `emit_success()` and `emit_error()` helper functions that print JSON to stdout
`uv sync` succeeds from the repo root without errors
`packages/cli_common/tests/test_models.py` exists with at least 3 tests covering ToolResponse and ToolError serialization
All tests pass: `uv run pytest packages/cli_common/tests/ -q` exits 0
userStories.notes: This is the foundation for all future packages. Keep cli_common minimal.

userStories.title: Scaffold ocr_tool package with models and configuration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the ocr_tool package scaffolded with its pyproject.toml, pydantic models for OCR requests/responses, and the engines module structure so that engine implementations can be added independently.
acceptanceCriteria: `packages/ocr_tool/pyproject.toml` exists with dependency on `cli_common` (as workspace dependency) and `Pillow>=10.0`
`packages/ocr_tool/src/ocr_tool/__init__.py` exists
`packages/ocr_tool/src/ocr_tool/models.py` defines pydantic models: `OcrRequest` (image_path, output_path, mode, model), `OcrResult` (text, source_image, output_path, mode, model_used)
`OcrRequest.mode` is a string literal union or enum with values `local` and `llm`
`packages/ocr_tool/src/ocr_tool/engines/__init__.py` exists
`packages/ocr_tool/src/ocr_tool/errors.py` defines `OcrError` exception subclassing `cli_common.errors.ToolException`
`uv sync` succeeds from repo root
`packages/ocr_tool/tests/test_models.py` exists with at least 3 tests covering model validation (valid input, missing required fields, invalid mode)
All tests pass: `uv run pytest packages/ocr_tool/tests/test_models.py -q` exits 0
userStories.notes: Do not implement engines or CLI yet. Models and package structure only.

userStories.title: Implement local OCR engine using easyocr
userStories.description: As a user, I want a local OCR engine that uses easyocr to extract text from images without requiring external API calls, so that I can run OCR offline.
acceptanceCriteria: `packages/ocr_tool/src/ocr_tool/engines/local.py` defines a function `extract_text_local(image_path: Path, model: str | None = None) -> str`
The function uses `easyocr` to perform OCR and returns the extracted text as a single string
The function validates the image path exists and raises `OcrError` with code `IMAGE_NOT_FOUND` if not
The function validates the image can be opened by Pillow and raises `OcrError` with code `INVALID_IMAGE` if not
`easyocr` is listed as a dependency in `packages/ocr_tool/pyproject.toml`
`packages/ocr_tool/tests/test_engines/test_local.py` exists with at least 4 tests: file-not-found error, invalid-image error, successful extraction (mocked easyocr), and text joining logic
All tests pass: `uv run pytest packages/ocr_tool/tests/test_engines/test_local.py -q` exits 0
userStories.notes: Mock easyocr in tests to avoid model downloads. Default language is English.

userStories.title: Implement LLM-based OCR engine
userStories.description: As a user, I want an LLM-based OCR engine that sends images to a multimodal LLM for text extraction, so that I can leverage advanced vision models for difficult images.
acceptanceCriteria: `packages/ocr_tool/src/ocr_tool/engines/llm.py` defines a function `extract_text_llm(image_path: Path, model: str | None = None) -> str`
The function reads the image, base64-encodes it, and sends it to the Anthropic API with a prompt requesting text extraction
The function reads the API key from `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` environment variable and raises `OcrError` with code `MISSING_API_KEY` if not set
The default model is `claude-sonnet-4-20250514` when `model` parameter is None
`anthropic` is listed as a dependency in `packages/ocr_tool/pyproject.toml`
The function validates the image path exists and the image is valid (same checks as local engine)
`packages/ocr_tool/tests/test_engines/test_llm.py` exists with at least 4 tests: missing API key error, file-not-found error, successful extraction (mocked API call), invalid-image error
All tests pass: `uv run pytest packages/ocr_tool/tests/test_engines/test_llm.py -q` exits 0
userStories.notes: All tests must mock the Anthropic API client. Never make real API calls in tests.

userStories.title: Implement OCR service orchestration layer
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a service layer that validates inputs, selects the correct engine based on mode, runs OCR, writes the output file, and returns a structured result.
acceptanceCriteria: `packages/ocr_tool/src/ocr_tool/service.py` defines a function `run_ocr(request: OcrRequest) -> OcrResult`
The function dispatches to `extract_text_local` when mode is `local` and `extract_text_llm` when mode is `llm`
When `output_path` is None, it defaults to `<image_stem>.txt` in the same directory as the input image
The function writes the extracted text to the output file
The function returns a fully populated `OcrResult` model

`packages/ocr_tool/tests/test_service.py` exists with at least 5 tests: local mode dispatch, llm mode dispatch, default output path generation, output file is written correctly, engine errors propagate as OcrError
All tests pass: `uv run pytest packages/ocr_tool/tests/test_service.py -q` exits 0
userStories.notes: Mock both engines in service tests. Test file writing with tmp_path fixture.

userStories.title: Implement typer CLI with extract command
userStories.description: As a user, I want a CLI command `ocr-tool extract` that accepts image path, output path, mode, and model options, runs OCR, and outputs structured JSON to stdout.
acceptanceCriteria: `packages/ocr_tool/src/ocr_tool/cli.py` defines a typer app with an `extract` command
The `extract` command accepts: `--image` (required, Path), `--output` (optional, Path), `--mode` (default `local`), `--model` (optional str)
On success, stdout contains JSON matching `{"ok": true, "result": {"text": ..., "source_image": ..., "output_path": ..., "mode": ..., "model_used": ...}}`
On error, stdout contains JSON matching `{"ok": false, "error": {"code": ..., "message": ..., "details": ...}}`
The CLI entry point is configured in `pyproject.toml` under `[project.scripts]` as `ocr-tool = "ocr_tool.cli:app"`
No decorative output, emojis, or colors are printed to stdout
`packages/ocr_tool/tests/test_cli.py` exists with at least 4 tests: help text works, missing image errors, successful extract (mocked service), invalid mode errors
All tests pass: `uv run pytest packages/ocr_tool/tests/test_cli.py -q` exits 0
userStories.notes: Use cli_common.io helpers for JSON output. Use typer.testing.CliRunner for CLI tests.

userStories.title: Add linting, type checking, and integration tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the entire codebase to pass ruff, mypy, and have integration-level tests so that code quality is enforced and the end-to-end flow is verified.
acceptanceCriteria: `uv run ruff check packages/cli_common/ packages/ocr_tool/` exits 0 with no errors
`uv run ruff format --check packages/cli_common/ packages/ocr_tool/` exits 0 (code is formatted)
`uv run mypy packages/cli_common/src/ packages/ocr_tool/src/` exits 0 with no errors
All existing unit tests still pass: `uv run pytest packages/ -q` exits 0
`ruff`, `mypy` are listed as dev dependencies in the root or relevant pyproject.toml
A `packages/ocr_tool/tests/test_integration.py` exists that tests the full CLI flow end-to-end with a real tiny test image (a 1x1 pixel PNG created in the test fixture) and mocked engines
The integration test verifies: JSON output is valid, output file is created, exit code is 0
userStories.notes: Fix any lint/type errors found. Add py.typed marker files if needed for mypy.
